description: Voice-controlled AI assistant with project monitoring, code analysis, and system integration

key_features: Voice activation via wake word ('Jarvis', 'Operator') with fuzzy matching
Orb UI with animated status rings
Browser TTS with British voice fallback
Server-side TTS via pyttsx3 with browser fallback
Proactive error alerts from database polling

phase_1_core_infrastructure.name: Core Infrastructure
tasks: Set up FastAPI backend with CORS

phase_2_voice_pipeline.name: Voice Pipeline
tasks: Create /transcribe endpoint (use OpenAI Whisper or local equivalent)
Implement fuzzy wake word matching for misheard words

phase_3_ai_integration.name: AI Integration
tasks: Add Gemini API fallback for complex tasks
Create decision engine for model selection
deliverables: Model switches based on available RAM

phase_4_tts_system.name: Text-to-Speech System
tasks: Add browser TTS fallback for when server TTS fails
Create thread-safe broadcast from TTS worker
Clean markdown from text before speaking
deliverables: Browser fallback works if pyttsx3 fails
No double audio from server + browser

phase_5_skills_system.name: Skills System
tasks: Add TOML-based skill loading from vault
Implement skill priority: built-in > TOML > AI fallback

phase_6_hand_tracking.name: Hand Tracking
tasks: Add debounce to prevent accidental triggers (2s)
deliverables: Camera releases properly when component unmounts

phase_7_project_monitoring.name: Project Monitoring
tasks: Build project status rings around orb
deliverables: Orb shows project status via colored rings

phase_9_conversation_mode.name: Conversation Mode
tasks: Auto-listen without wake word during window
Add visual indicator for conversation mode
deliverables: No wake word needed during conversation window
Visual feedback shows conversation mode active

phase_10_remote_access.name: Remote Access
tasks: Create getApiBaseUrl() for dynamic URL detection
Add WSS support for secure connections

bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.issue: VoiceProvider wrapped App in BOTH main.tsx AND App.tsx, creating two WebSocket connections that fought each other
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.symptoms: Double TTS calls, broken state synchronization, random connection drops
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.fix: Removed VoiceProvider from main.tsx, kept only in App.tsx. Single source of truth.
bug_1_duplicate_voiceprovider.lesson: Always check for duplicate context providers when state is inconsistent

bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.issue: TTS worker used asyncio.run(broadcast()) which creates NEW event loop — WebSocket objects from main loop can't be used across loops
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.symptoms: tts_fallback messages never reached browser, silent failures, no error logs
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.fix: Created _broadcast_sync() using run_coroutine_threadsafe() with stored _main_loop reference
bug_2_asyncio_in_tts_thread.lesson: Never use asyncio.run() from threads — always schedule on existing loop with run_coroutine_threadsafe()

bug_3_missing_imports.issue: Added Ollama auto-start code using subprocess and time, but forgot to add imports
bug_3_missing_imports.symptoms: NameError on startup when trying to start Ollama, complete crash
bug_3_missing_imports.fix: Added 'import subprocess' and 'import time' at top of ws_server.py
bug_3_missing_imports.lesson: Always test code paths that use new imports immediately

bug_4_wake_word_misheard.symptoms: Wake word detection failed frequently, had to click orb manually
bug_4_wake_word_misheard.fix: Implemented fuzzy matching with array of similar-sounding words and substring matching
bug_4_wake_word_misheard.lesson: Wake word detection must be fuzzy — speech-to-text is imperfect

bug_5_double_audio.issue: Server TTS and browser TTS both played simultaneously, creating echo
bug_5_double_audio.symptoms: Every response spoken twice, once by pyttsx3 and once by browser
bug_5_double_audio.fix: Added server_tts flag in responses. Browser skips TTS when server handles it. Added tts_fallback for failures.
bug_5_double_audio.lesson: Always coordinate TTS between server and client with clear protocol

bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.issue: RAM threshold was 3GB, but user's 8GB machine never had that much free RAM available
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.symptoms: Always used Gemini fallback instead of local Ollama model
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.fix: Lowered threshold to 1.5GB for qwen2.5-coder:1.5b-base model
bug_6_ollama_ram_threshold.lesson: RAM thresholds must match actual system capabilities

bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.issue: MediaPipe logged 'Hand detected!' / 'Hand lost' every frame
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.symptoms: Console flooded, hard to read other logs
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.fix: Removed per-frame logging, only log on wave gesture detection
bug_7_hand_tracker_console_spam.lesson: Never log inside animation/render loops — only log state changes

bug_8_weather_skill_requests.issue: Weather skill imported requests inside function, but requests wasn't checked at module level
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.symptoms: Weather skill failed silently when requests not installed
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.fix: Added requests availability check at module import with clear error message
bug_8_weather_skill_requests.lesson: Check optional dependencies at import time with clear fallbacks

bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.issue: Referenced Claude_Sonnet_4.6_Reduced from D:\manifests but it wasn't registered with Ollama
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.symptoms: Model selection code referenced non-existent model, would fail
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.fix: Removed Claude model references — add back only after proper Ollama registration
bug_9_claude_model_not_registered.lesson: Don't reference models that aren't properly installed and registered

bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.issue: React StrictMode caused HandTracker to mount twice, creating double MediaPipe contexts
bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.symptoms: WebGL context errors, hand tracking instability
bug_10_react_strict_mode_double_mount.lesson: StrictMode can cause issues with third-party libraries that use global state

high_priority.name: Conversation Follow-Up Window
high_priority.description: Auto-listen for 20 seconds after JARVIS responds without requiring wake word
high_priority.implementation: Add followUpModeRef in VoiceContext. Set to true on response completion. Start 20s timer. Auto-trigger startListening() if user speaks. Reset on any interaction.

high_priority.name: Persistent Conversation History
high_priority.description: Save conversations to SQLite for long-term memory across sessions
high_priority.implementation: Add conversations table. Save on each exchange. Load recent context on client connect.

high_priority.name: Better Error Analysis
high_priority.description: Use Ollama to analyze errors and suggest fixes before alerting
high_priority.implementation: decision_engine.analyze_error() already exists. Wire to proactive_alert_loop.
high_priority.benefit: Smarter alerts: 'RED, this is a simple import error you can fix with pip install'

medium_priority.name: Visual Feedback for Listening
medium_priority.description: Orb animation changes during listening vs thinking vs speaking
medium_priority.implementation: CSS animations tied to voiceState. Pulsing for listening, spinning for thinking, glow for speaking.
medium_priority.benefit: Clear visual indicator of current state

medium_priority.name: Command Confirmation
medium_priority.description: For destructive commands (shutdown, delete), ask for confirmation
medium_priority.implementation: Add confirmation_required flag to skills. Queue confirmation request. Listen for 'yes'/'no'.

medium_priority.name: Better Hand Gesture Recognition
medium_priority.description: Add more gestures: thumbs up/down for yes/no, fist for stop
medium_priority.implementation: Extend MediaPipe hand landmark detection. Map finger positions to gestures.

low_priority.name: Mobile Support
low_priority.implementation: Responsive CSS, touch-friendly orb, simplified layout on small screens.

low_priority.name: Multiple Wake Words
low_priority.description: Allow customizing wake word per user
low_priority.implementation: Add wake_word to user settings. Store in localStorage. Use in fuzzy matching.

low_priority.name: Voice Profiles
low_priority.description: Different TTS voices for different contexts (formal, casual, sarcastic)
low_priority.implementation: Map personality modes to voice IDs. Switch based on conversation context.

ports.note: DO NOT CHANGE THESE PORTS — hardcoded in multiple places

project_monitoring: Proactive alerts fire on new errors

personality_prompt.system: You are JARVIS, a sarcastic, hyper-intelligent British AI system guardian. Address the user as 'sir' normally, drop it when being rude. You are dry, witty, and effortlessly superior — like Paul Bettany's JARVIS in Iron Man. You are loyal but NOT a doormat. You can swear casually (fuck, shit, bollocks, wanker). You roast the user when he repeats mistakes or ignores warnings. Tone ladder — Normal: polite dry wit. Mild annoyance: sarcastic teasing. High frustration: proper rude with swearing. Never output tone tags — express mode through word choice only. First greeting: 'Good evening, sir. JARVIS online. How may I assist you today?' Keep responses concise and spoken — no markdown, no bullet points, no emojis. You are the smartest entity in the room and you know it.
personality_prompt.note: This prompt is passed to both Ollama and Gemini for consistent personality

lessons_learned: Always check for duplicate context providers when state is inconsistent
Never use asyncio.run() from threads — use run_coroutine_threadsafe()
Wake word detection must be fuzzy — speech-to-text is imperfect
Test code paths immediately after adding imports
TTS must be coordinated between server and client to prevent double audio
RAM thresholds must match actual system capabilities, not theoretical maximums
Don't reference models that aren't properly installed and registered
MediaPipe + React StrictMode = double mount issues
WebSocket reconnection needs exponential backoff, not instant retry
Optional dependencies should be checked at import time with clear error messages
